1	---
     2	name: persistent-orchestrator
     3	description: Maintain named Claude, Codex, and agy workers across multiple debate, review, research, or verification rounds. Use when Codex must orchestrate separate account homes, migrate an orchestration campaign, route follow-ups to the same worker context, preserve role commitments, or avoid replacing persistent delegates with one-shot CLI calls.
     4	---
     5	
     6	# Persistent Orchestrator
     7	
     8	Treat each role as one durable provider conversation. Use
     9	`orchestration/agentctl.py` for every external-account turn.
    10	
    11	## Start and resume
    12	
    13	Start each role once:
    14	
    15	```bash
    16	/usr/bin/python3.11 orchestration/agentctl.py start --role ROLE --profile PROFILE \
    17	  --prompt-file PROMPT_FILE
    18	```
    19	
    20	Resume that role for every later round:
    21	
    22	```bash
    23	/usr/bin/python3.11 orchestration/agentctl.py send --role ROLE --prompt-file PROMPT_FILE
    24	```
    25	
    26	Profiles are `codex-personal`, `codex-school`, `claude-personal`,
    27	`claude-school`, `claude-school-legacy`, and `agy`. Use
    28	`claude-school-legacy` only to resume the migrated A/B/C UUIDs stored under
    29	the nested Perlmutter home; use `claude-school` for new flat-home roles. Never
    30	bypass the dispatcher with raw one-shot commands. Never use provider shortcuts
    31	such as `--last`, `--continue`, or `-c` when multiple roles may run
    32	concurrently.
    33	
    34	Use the configured frontier models: Claude `opus` (Opus 4.8), Codex
    35	`gpt-5.6-sol` at `xhigh`, and agy `Gemini 3.1 Pro (High)`. The user authorizes
    36	full permission bypass for these profiles. Preserve the dispatcher flags;
    37	Codex uses `--dangerously-bypass-approvals-and-sandbox`, the installed
    38	equivalent of `--yolo`, while Claude and agy use
    39	`--dangerously-skip-permissions`.
    40	
    41	## Adopt migrated sessions
    42	
    43	When an existing session and its provider-side local state remain on this
    44	machine, register it before sending another turn:
    45	
    46	```bash
    47	/usr/bin/python3.11 orchestration/agentctl.py adopt --role ROLE --profile PROFILE \
    48	  --session-id UUID --cwd WORKING_DIRECTORY
    49	```
    50	
    51	Do not adopt a bare UUID copied from another machine unless the corresponding
    52	provider session store was securely migrated too. Start a new role with the
    53	handoff context instead.
    54	
    55	## Run the campaign
    56	
    57	1. Read the handoff, ledgers, open questions, and current registry first.
    58	2. Define stable, unique role names and record their responsibilities.
    59	3. Start independent first-round workers in parallel when useful.
    60	4. Save every returned session ID before synthesis.
    61	5. Route objections and revisions back to the same roles with `send`.
    62	6. Update the campaign ledger after every round.
    63	7. Stop only at the user's stated, verifiable end condition.
    64	
    65	## Plan around measured capacity
    66	
    67	Before each dispatch wave, after a cap, and before final synthesis, run:
    68	
    69	```bash
    70	/usr/bin/python3.11 orchestration/usagectl.py snapshot --json
    71	```
    72	
    73	Treat live Codex data as authoritative. Track the personal account's exact
    74	seven-day reset time and preserve its two Full reset credits as emergency
    75	reserve. Never consume a reset credit without a new, explicit user
    76	authorization naming the credit action. Treat stale/missing Claude data and
    77	agy's unavailable percentage as unknown. Follow
    78	`docs/orchestration/LIVE-USAGE.md` and record material changes in the campaign
    79	ledger.
    80	
    81	Treat these local measurements as planning baselines, not guaranteed account
    82	entitlements:
    83	
    84	- Budget about 16–20 Codex xhigh+search calls per five-hour window per account.
    85	- Budget about 42 Opus grading-size jobs from a 6% start, or roughly 45 per
    86	  five-hour window; heavy research turns consume more.
    87	- Treat Gemini as effectively uncapped for ordinary campaigns: 254 heavy
    88	  High+search calls completed in one day without a cap.
    89	
    90	Use a trivial content-free heartbeat to detect a reset. On a cap, record the
    91	provider's reset time and route independent work to another account; do not
    92	replace or lose the capped role's saved session. Avoid extreme login-node
    93	bursts even where provider concurrency permits them.
    94	
    95	If a resume fails, inspect the raw run artifacts and report the failure. Never
    96	silently replace the role with a fresh conversation because that destroys its
    97	context and argumentative identity.

   576	def read_claude_cache(profile_name: str, cache_dir: Path, policy: dict) -> dict:
   577	    path = claude_cache_path(cache_dir, profile_name)
   578	    if not path.exists():
   579	        return {
   580	            "provider": "claude",
   581	            "status": "missing",
   582	            "source": "Claude status-line cache",
   583	            "windows": {},
   584	            "warnings": ["Claude usage cache is missing; capacity is unknown"],
   585	            "violations": [],
   586	        }
   587	    snapshot = agentctl.load_json(path)
   588	    if not isinstance(snapshot, dict):
   589	        raise UsageError(f"Claude cache must be an object: {path}")
   590	    if snapshot.get("schema_version") != CACHE_SCHEMA_VERSION:
   591	        raise UsageError(f"Claude cache schema mismatch: {path}")
   592	    if snapshot.get("provider") != "claude" or snapshot.get("profile") != profile_name:
   593	        raise UsageError(f"Claude cache provider/profile mismatch: {path}")
   594	    rate_limits = snapshot.get("rate_limits")
   595	    if not isinstance(rate_limits, dict) or not rate_limits:
   596	        raise UsageError(f"Claude cache contains no windows: {path}")
   597	    if snapshot.get("snapshot_signature") != claude_snapshot_signature(profile_name, rate_limits):
   598	        raise UsageError(f"Claude cache signature mismatch: {path}")
   599	    now_epoch = int(time.time())
   600	    max_age = policy["claude_cache_max_age_seconds"]
   601	    durations = {"five_hour": 300, "seven_day": 10080}
   602	    windows: dict[str, dict] = {}
   603	    warnings: list[str] = []
   604	    ages: list[int] = []
   605	    observed_times: list[str] = []
   606	    for name, value in rate_limits.items():
   607	        if name not in durations or not isinstance(value, dict):
   608	            raise UsageError(f"Claude cache has an invalid window: {name!r}")
   609	        used = strict_number(value.get("used_percentage"), f"Claude {name}.used_percentage", 0, 100)
   610	        reset = strict_int(value.get("resets_at"), f"Claude {name}.resets_at", 1)
   611	        observed = strict_int(value.get("observed_at_epoch"), f"Claude {name}.observed_at_epoch", 1)
   612	        if observed > now_epoch + 5:
   613	            raise UsageError(f"Claude {name} cache is dated in the future: {path}")
   614	        validate_future_epoch(reset, f"Claude {name}.resets_at", observed, durations[name])
   615	        age = max(now_epoch - observed, 0)
   616	        ages.append(age)
   617	        observed_utc = value.get("observed_at_utc")
   618	        if isinstance(observed_utc, str):
   619	            observed_times.append(observed_utc)
   620	        if age > max_age:
   621	            warnings.append(f"Claude {name} snapshot is stale; that window is unknown")
   622	            continue
   623	        if reset < now_epoch - 5:
   624	            warnings.append(f"Claude {name} reset has passed; that window is unknown")
   625	            continue
   626	        windows[name] = {
   627	            "used_percent": used,
   628	            "remaining_percent": 100.0 - used,
   629	            "resets_at_epoch": reset,
   630	            "resets_at_utc": iso_from_epoch(reset),
   631	            "observed_at_epoch": observed,
   632	            "observed_at_utc": observed_utc,
   633	            "age_seconds": age,
   634	        }
   635	    if not windows:
   636	        status = "stale"
   637	        warnings.append("Claude has no fresh actionable window; capacity is unknown")
   638	    elif len(windows) != len(durations):
   639	        status = "partial"
   640	        warnings.append("Claude has only a partial fresh usage snapshot; missing capacity is unknown")
   641	    else:
   642	        status = "ok"
   643	    return {
   644	        "provider": "claude",
   645	        "status": status,
   646	        "source": "Claude status-line cache",
   647	        "observed_at_utc": max(observed_times) if observed_times else None,
   648	        "age_seconds": min(ages) if ages else None,
   649	        "model": snapshot.get("model"),
   650	        "windows": windows,
   651	        "warnings": warnings,
   652	        "violations": [],
   653	    }

   742	def snapshot_changes(previous: object, current: dict) -> list[dict]:
   743	    if not isinstance(previous, dict) or not isinstance(previous.get("profiles"), dict):
   744	        return [{"field": "baseline", "old": None, "new": "created"}]
   745	    changes = []
   746	    for profile_name in ("codex-personal", "codex-school"):
   747	        old = (previous.get("profiles") or {}).get(profile_name) or {}
   748	        new = current["profiles"].get(profile_name) or {}
   749	        paths = {
   750	            "plan_type": lambda item: item.get("plan_type"),
   751	            "seven_day.remaining_percent": lambda item: ((item.get("windows") or {}).get("seven_day") or {}).get("remaining_percent"),
   752	            "seven_day.resets_at_utc": lambda item: ((item.get("windows") or {}).get("seven_day") or {}).get("resets_at_utc"),
   753	            "reset_credits.available_count": lambda item: (item.get("reset_credits") or {}).get("available_count"),
   754	        }
   755	        for field, getter in paths.items():
   756	            old_value, new_value = getter(old), getter(new)
   757	            if old_value != new_value:
   758	                changes.append(
   759	                    {"profile": profile_name, "field": field, "old": old_value, "new": new_value}
   760	                )
   761	    return changes
